python-v2.title: Python for Beginners
python-v2.description: This course teaches the core foundations of Python: running code, values, variables, input, strings, conditions, and loops.
python-v2.moreComingSoon: More Python for Beginners lessons are coming soon.

python-v2-0.title: Getting Started with Python
python-v2-0.description: Start like a real beginner programmer: run short Python programs, read the output, and build a tiny message app step by step.
outcomes: Run Python code in the browser editor and read the output clearly
Use print statements, expressions, and comments with confidence
Finish short programs that say exactly what you want
why: Gives learners a smooth first win instead of throwing them into syntax all at once.
Builds the habits they need for every later project in the course.

python-v2-1.title: Variables, Input, and Strings
python-v2-1.description: Turn simple print programs into interactive ones that remember values, accept input, and print clean text back to the user.
outcomes: Store, update, and reuse values with clear variable names
Read user input and convert it when the program needs numbers
Format friendly output with string methods and f-strings
why: Makes programs feel interactive instead of fixed and scripted.
Prepares learners to build small profile, message, and formatter projects.

python-v2-2.title: Conditions and Logic
python-v2-2.description: Teach programs to make decisions by comparing values, checking special cases, and choosing the right branch.
outcomes: Write clear if, elif, and else chains
Use comparisons and boolean logic to control program behavior
Build decision rules that match real beginner scenarios
why: Shows learners how code can react instead of always doing the same thing.
Prepares them for projects like checkers, classifiers, and simple rule-based tools.

python-v2-3.title: Loops and Iteration
python-v2-3.description: Bring the earlier skills together by repeating actions, processing text and numbers, and finishing with a small gradebook capstone.
outcomes: Use for loops and while loops to process repeated work
Trace counters, totals, and loop state step by step
Apply range, break, and continue inside a complete final project
why: Helps learners move from one-line tasks to programs that handle several pieces of data.
Ends the course with a project that feels like a real beginner tool, not just isolated drills.

python-v2-0-setup-and-first-programs.title: Setup and First Programs
python-v2-0-setup-and-first-programs.description: Meet Python, run your first short programs, and learn how printed output, comments, and basic fixes work together.

python-v2-0-values-and-expressions.title: Values and Expressions
python-v2-0-values-and-expressions.description: Work with numbers, strings, and expressions while learning how to spot and fix simple Python errors.

python-v2-0-module-project.title: Module Project
python-v2-0-module-project.description: A club leader needs a simple welcome board for the first meetup. Build a short Python program that prints clear messages in the right order.
bullets: Turn basic print statements into one small finished program
Use exact output, ordering, and simple expressions together
Finish module 0 with a real beginner project instead of isolated drills

python-v2-1-variables-and-assignment.title: Variables and Assignment
python-v2-1-variables-and-assignment.description: Learn how programs store information, read input, and avoid the beginner mistakes that break variable-based code.

python-v2-1-string-foundations.title: String Foundations
python-v2-1-string-foundations.description: Practice slicing text, cleaning strings, and formatting readable output for small interactive programs.

python-v2-1-module-project.title: Module Project
python-v2-1-module-project.description: A tutor wants a quick profile card generator for new students. Build a small program that reads input, cleans text, and prints a polished profile.
bullets: Store and reuse values with clear variable names
Read learner input and format it cleanly
Combine variables, input, string methods, and f-strings in one project

python-v2-2-conditional-basics.title: Conditional Basics
python-v2-2-conditional-basics.description: Build the core decision-making tools in Python: comparisons, truth values, indentation, and branching code.

python-v2-2-combining-conditions.title: Combining Conditions
python-v2-2-combining-conditions.description: Combine conditions, handle empty values, and check special cases in the order that makes your rules clear.

python-v2-2-module-project.title: Module Project
python-v2-2-module-project.description: A study coach needs a simple checker that explains student status clearly. Build a small rules-based program that handles missing data, invalid values, and score ranges.
bullets: Use if, elif, and else to choose the right message
Check special cases before broader score rules
Finish the module with a practical decision-making program

python-v2-3-for-loops.title: For Loops
python-v2-3-for-loops.description: Use for loops to walk through text, count in patterns, and build totals and counters that grow over time.

python-v2-3-while-loops.title: While Loops
python-v2-3-while-loops.description: Learn how while loops keep going until a condition changes, then use that skill in the course capstone.

python-v2-3-course-capstone.title: Course Capstone
python-v2-3-course-capstone.description: Bring the whole course together in a final gradebook project that reads repeated input, tracks results, and prints a report a tutor can actually use.
bullets: Read several inputs and process them with loops
Use totals, comparisons, formatting, and branching together
Finish the course with one complete beginner-friendly program
